name: terry
description: A collection of Claude Code skills for general software-engineering work — each a self-contained, named methodology you invoke by intent. `terry` (agent-fleet orchestration AND a producer-consumer task queue with backpressure — a long-running manager loop that drives parallel worker agents across isolated git worktrees, coordinated through a JSON priority-queue, dispatching only into free capacity so concurrency stays capped to avoid OOM and pace token spend on a budget, with manager-only merges into an integration branch, distributed-VCS style), `andrej` (the empirical observe→analyze→act iteration loop — instrument before you hypothesize, one-variable bounded experiments gated on a scalar metric with keep-or-revert and an append-only ledger), `brendan` (Brendan Gregg's systems-performance methodology — the USE method, the 60-second checklist, TSA / workload-characterization / drill-down / latency methods, plus the per-resource tool checklists for CPU/memory/network/disk and GPUs via nvidia-smi / DCGM), `sherlock` (root-cause-by-elimination debugging — 'eliminate the impossible, whatever remains is the truth': the complete suspect list, falsify-don't-confirm, eliminate-by-evidence-not-assumption, and the negative-evidence / zero-survivors traps), `jamie` (reproduce-before-you-believe bug triage — turn a report into a minimal reliable repro by matching the reporter's conditions, quantify reliability, and return a CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / BUSTED verdict), and `ronald` (controlled A/B/n experiments across models and prompts — Ronald Fisher's experimental design: one variable, paired test cases, N replicates for stochastic noise, a blinded judge, a pre-registered metric and decision rule, and an honest beyond-the-noise verdict; starts with a Claude adapter, extensible to Codex/Gemini). Built for long-running, data-driven agent work but useful for everyday debugging, performance, triage, and model/prompt evaluation.
